title: Wholesome Umami Turkish Shrimp Flatbread
description: A crunchy, long-fermented sourdough flatbread topped with umami-rich shrimp and sumac-spiced aromatics, traditionally served in a Korean brass bowl during the monsoon season.
instructions: Mix the rye flour, sourdough starter, salt, and water into a shaggy dough.
Ferment the dough at room temperature for twelve hours to ensure a low-glycemic profile and deep flavor.
Preheat your oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit with a baking stone inside.
Sauté the shrimp, tomato paste, and Aleppo pepper in one tablespoon of olive oil until just pink.
Stretch the fermented dough into a thin circle on a piece of parchment paper.
Spread the shrimp mixture and minced olives evenly over the surface of the dough.
Slide the flatbread onto the hot stone and bake for twelve minutes until the edges are crunchy.
Remove from the oven and drizzle with the remaining olive oil and fresh parsley.
Slice the flatbread into wedges and place them into a chilled Korean brass bowl for serving.
Serve immediately while the crust is crisp to contrast the humid monsoon air.
